narrative.context: A teammate’s work affected your project timeline. You feel frustrated but also nervous about giving feedback.
narrative.prompt: How do you use these emotions to choose effective words?

options.label: Use emotion to choose clarity + respect
options.description: Let frustration signal the impact; let nervousness signal the need for care. Use specifics: behavior, impact, request.

ei.rationale: Emotions guide both content and tone planning—supports clear thinking and effective communication.

options.label: Hint indirectly
options.description: Make vague comments so it doesn’t feel confrontational.

ei.rationale: Reduces discomfort, but emotion is not used to support clear thinking and problem-solving.

options.label: Delay until you explode
options.description: Avoid the talk until frustration builds.

ei.rationale: Emotions are not used constructively; thinking and timing worsen.

options.label: Criticize their character
options.description: Say they are lazy or unreliable.

ei.rationale: Emotion turns into blame; undermines problem-solving and relationship.
